title: 2 killed in crash on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth town line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people died in a two-vehicle crash on Route 6 near the Westport-Dartmouth line, Friday evening.
Victims identified as Tristan Bedient of Acushnet and Kate Aldrich of New Bedford were in a Toyota Camry sedan.
The incident is under investigation by Massachusetts State Police and local police departments.

title: Bus driver strike ends in Merrimack Valley after nearly 2 weeks
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Merrimack Valley Transit workers end 10-day strike after securing a one-year agreement with a $4 hourly wage increase.
The agreement preserves Saturday overtime and commits to future negotiations, resuming fare-free bus service across affected towns.
The contract followed six months of negotiations, addressing concerns over proposed changes to weekend shifts and compensation.

title: 5 takeaways as red-hot Red Sox rally past Mets, 3-2, for 9th straight victory
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox beat Mets 3-2 in extra innings for ninth consecutive win, ending the first half of the season strong.
The team has won 14 of their last 16 games, allowing two runs or fewer in the last six.
Boston enters All-Star break with an impressive road sweep, marking their best win streak into the break since 1977.

title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after a four-night stint at Fenway Park.
Kahan became the fourth artist to receive this honor, joining Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and Zac Brown Band.
Highlights of Kahan's Fenway shows included fireworks and appearances by sports figures Rob Gronkowski and David Ortiz.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from Cash App in a $45 million multistate settlement over deceptive practices.
Block Inc. allegedly misled users about Cash App's safety and failed to protect them from fraud as reported by the AG.
The settlement requires Cash App to improve security, discontinue high-risk promotions, and enhance customer support.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old from Tyngsborough allegedly used a police boat on Lake Mascuppic, activating its emergency lights.
He is accused of impersonating an officer by attempting to enforce local boating rules before returning the boat.
Authorities, in collaboration with Massachusetts Environmental Police, plan to issue a summons for a court appearance.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels participated in Sail Boston 2026's Parade of Sail on Saturday.
The USS Constitution led ships from over 20 countries, arriving from New York and docking at Boston waterfront locations.
Thousands gathered to witness the final stop of the Sail250 tour, celebrating Boston's maritime history.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox drafted shortstop Jake Schaffner at No. 20 in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, played for UNC, hitting .356/.467/.552 with 50 RBI in 2026.
With a .987 fielding percentage, Schaffner was noted for his speed and defensive reliability at shortstop.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston Grand Parade of Sail 2026 features historic ships, including the USS Constitution, along Boston's waterfront.
Carrie Martin celebrates her nephew's participation in the parade aboard the schooner Adventure, emphasizing maritime education for youth.
Sail Boston 2026 marks the event's 250th anniversary with vessels like the Grand Banks replica Bluenose Two and the Spirit of Bermuda.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello was named the midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for his poor pitching performance.
Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts, leading to his demotion to Triple-A Worcester.
Despite struggles as a starter, Bello excelled in relief with a 0.71 ERA and a 0.91 WHIP.
